chapter_goal: Show how residues and pole order translate the Hodge filtration of a hypersurface into computable graded algebra.
checks: toy Jacobian quotient has expected monomial basis
lab: Change the degree of a Fermat-type polynomial and compare the available residue monomials.
library_routes: SymPy for small polynomial quotient checks
pitfalls: treating pole order as the same as cohomological degree
using Torelli conclusions outside generic hypotheses
proof_moves: identify Hodge pieces by pole order
translate the infinitesimal period map into graded multiplication
visual_focus: Nested pole-order bands feed a Jacobian-ring multiplication grid for hypersurface IVHS.
